*What is Modal?!*
A very quick primer on the difference between Kyozo and Modal.
*Kyozo is the architecture for creatives to build communities* — around themselves, or the groups they manage. It is the technology extension of their human skills (not the replacement).  We know first hand that it is desperately need!
Kyozo will be the central point of their community-building efforts. We’ll make their life easier so they can deliver meaningful human experiences and build great communities. And once they’ve done that, we’ll help them monetise — so that their journey is rewarded and sustainable.
*MODAL* is a new content series I’ve been working on for the last six months. It may be new to some of you, so here’s what it is. Modal helps people understand and break down the big themes our industry covers: music, context, performance art, dance. 
It’s a content series designed to help people appreciate the world we’re moving into — and, hopefully, raise the value of art and human creativity in the process.
The two will work together. I’ll be producing content two or three times a week — episodes about music, about creativity — constantly driving people to Kyozo. 
That’s the distinction for now. Think of Modal as my product, my community, my idea — and I’m using Kyozo to distribute it.

Apple is responding to build 7 (still attached to v0.0.1 — we never swapped to build 8). Their complaint is real and specific: they tested the app and couldn't see any age-rated content in the feed, so they concluded the labeling/filtering mechanism doesn't exist.
What's actually happening
Our code for 1.2.1(a) is correct and functional:
AgeRating enum on Post with everyone and adult
"17+" pill badge on PostCard when post.isAgeRestricted
Feed filter in community_screen.dart that hides adult posts if viewer's declared age < 17
But — no posts in Firestore have ageRating: 'adult'. Every post defaults to everyone, so the reviewer signs in, browses the feed, sees no 17+ badges anywhere, and reasonably concludes "the mechanism doesn't exist."
The mechanism is there. There's just nothing to mechanize against. That's the gap Apple is pointing at.
Two-part fix — needed before resubmission
Part 1 (data): mark some Firestore posts as adult-rated
This is the critical fix. You need at least 2-3 visible adult-rated posts in the willer community feed so the reviewer can:
See a "17+" pill on the post card (proves identification mechanism)
Sign in with an under-17 declared birth year and watch those posts disappear from the feed (proves filtering mechanism)
Open Firestore Console → blogs collection → pick 2-3 existing posts in the willer community → add field ageRating: "adult" (string) on each. Or do it via the admin app you have.
Make them posts the reviewer is likely to actually scroll past — something near the top of the feed, with a real image or video, not stub content.
Part 2 (UX): make the mechanism more discoverable
Right now the mechanism is invisible unless a user happens to scroll past an adult post. Let me add a Settings → Safety → Content preferences screen that displays the current age-appropriate status — gives Apple's reviewer a place to explicitly verify the mechanism exists even without scrolling.
